templates.name: Can you use the sensitive dip base and sensitive dip top coat with a gel polish?
templates.content: Not exactly, the Top Coat is Gel, so it can be used over the top of gel polish in theory, but you would need the Gel Base rather than the Sensitive Base.

templates.name: Can I use the Gel Thinner with the sensitive liquids?
templates.content: The Gel Thinner is not compatible with our Sensitive Liquids.

templates.name: Are Step 1 and Step 3 in the Sensitive Dip Powder Liquid Set interchangeable?
templates.content: No, the Sensitive Base and Sensitive Gel Top Coat aren't interchangeable in the Sensitive Liquid Set. We recommend following the instructions and using the steps in order for the best result.

templates.name: Will my Sun Changers work when using the Sensitive Liquid line?
templates.content: Yes, sun changers will still work. Please note they do still require a UV index of about 4 to see the change.

templates.name: Will color payoff be affected when using the Sensitive Liquid Set?
templates.content: Although we did not experience this during testing, it's possible the use of a UV/LED light may affect the color payoff slightly.

templates.name: Do Sensitive Liquids require a UV light?
templates.content: Yes! The Sensitive Liquid Gel Top Coat does require a UV/LED lamp to cure. You can purchase our UV/LED Sensitive Liquid Bundle here: https://www.revelnail.com/products/sensitive-dip-powder-liquid-bundle-with-uv-led-nail-lamp

templates.name: What wattage is required for the Sensitive Gel Top Coat?
templates.content: The Sensitive Gel Top Coat requires a 36-48 watt UV/LED lamp.

templates.name: Can the Sensitive Gel Top Coat be substituted with Glass Shield or the Tack-Free Gel Top Coat?
templates.content: Yes, although for best results we recommend using all three Sensitive Liquids in conjunction to prevent "dip flu" symptoms.

templates.name: Can I use the Sensitive Dip Powder Liquid Set if I am allergic to Gel?
templates.content: Please consult your physician. Our gel top coat is HEMA free but this doesn't guarantee you won't have a reaction.

templates.name: Can I use my EZ Care or Professional Finish Gel in place of the Sensitive Gel Top Coat?
templates.content: Our Sensitive Dip Powder Liquid Set was created to achieve beautiful manis without experiencing "dip flu." Although swapping out the Sensitive Gel Top Coat for Finish Gel may work, it wasn't designed to be used this way. If you use Finish Gel in place of the HEMA-Free gel you may still experience "dip flu." For best results, please follow all instructions thoroughly.

templates.name: Restock - Limited Edition
templates.content:
If something is from our main collection and is out of stock, then it will come back, but unfortunately, we don't know when. Something like a "Last Call" item will not come back in stock, once it sells out it sells out. Certain limited edition kits and items like Beach, Please, or the Fall Drop In Collection are only available for a particular season like Summer or Fall. We are trying hard to prioritize popular shades and items and are producing and restocking items as fast as we can.
 
Want to know when an item is back in stock? You can sign up to be notified on our website! Go to the sold-out product's page on revelnail.com. Here you will see an option that says, "notify me when available." Click here, and you will be added to the email list! If we don't expect to restock a particular item, you will not see a Notify Me option due to seasonality and high demand. Hope this helps!

templates.name: Can I use the removal kit with the gel nail and dip powder?
templates.content: Absolutely! The removal kit can be used with both the gel nail and the dip powder.

templates.name: Liquid size of the cuticle oil pen
templates.content: It's a 15ml pen.

templates.name: How do you use the Gel Thinner?
templates.content: You should use the Gel Thinner after every single manicure in both your Pro Base and Finish Gel. If you wait until your liquids already start to thicken to use it, you'll find that your liquids have already been damaged beyond repair. The best way to prolong the life of your liquids with Gel Thinner would be to add 2-5 drops of it to both your bottles of Pro Base and Finish Gel after every single manicure.

templates.name: What's the name of the shade I received as a free gift?
templates.content: If the color is a freebie, it's not part of our original collection and doesn't have a name or can be purchased. I apologize for the inconvenience.

templates.name: Will I lose my points after my tier is reset?
templates.content: Your points will not expire even if the Tier is reset.

templates.name: What is chrome?
templates.content: Chrome is a reflective high polished shiny manicure style that is absolutely eye catching, and not to mention addictive! Although our Mirror Chrome can be used alone, we do recommend that a base layer of D7 Barbara be dipped first. The beauty in our mirror chrome collection is that you get two manicure styles in one. It can be used with a gel top coat and UV light to create the high polished chrome effect or it can be dipped just like any other powder to achieve a glittery accent. You can also watch our tutorial on "How to Chrome" via this link: https://www.revelnail.com/pages/how-to-use-mirror-chrome?_pos=1&_sid=784b57d0c&_ss=r

templates.name: What is the difference between lacquer and nail polish?
templates.content: Nail Lacquer is the same as the regular Nail Polish. You don't need a UV lamp and just have to wait until they are fully dry. Meanwhile, the Gel Polish needs a UV lamp to cure.

templates.name: Difference between the EZ Care, Professional Liquids & Gel?
templates.content: The difference between EZ Care and Professional liquids is the drying time. EZ's have a longer drying time than Professional liquids to give beginners more time to work with their manicure before it dries. Professional liquids are designed for the more experienced dipper, especially nail techs who need to work fast with their customers. The Gel method involves our Gel Base Coat and Tack-Free Gel Topcoat. Using it requires a UV lamp to cure the gel and set the manicure. You can use the Gel method with our Dip Powders, however, the finished look would be slightly different as the Gels have a different formula than the Dipping Liquids.

templates.name: Are your cuticle oil pens refillable?
templates.content: Unfortunately, the cuticle oil pens aren't refillable.

templates.name: I get a reaction when I use your products / I feel sick
templates.content: If you experience cold-like symptoms such as runny nose, stuffy nose, coughing, etc, then there's a possibility that you're having a reaction to an ingredient in the Pro Base and Finish Gel. Reactions can vary from mild to a more severe one. There are a few things you can do to help minimize your reaction. First, the area should be well ventilated when using the product, this will minimize the fumes. Consider a small fan pointing away from you as you apply. Second, watch that you don't hunch over the product to see better - getting very close to the open bottle to see your nails better will cause you to inhale more of it. This is one of the most common reasons women develop a reaction. A bright light over your workspace will help with this. There are also masks available that will minimize your reaction. If you're experiencing a reaction, please consult your doctor. Your health is the most important thing to us. If you feel like using Dip Powder is causing your body to have a negative reaction, please stop using it.

templates.name: Are your products safe to use while pregnant?
templates.content: Our products should be safe to use while pregnant! Plenty of our employees have been pregnant and have continued to get their nails done throughout their pregnancies. I would of course, recommend checking with your doctor and only do what you feel comfortable doing. If you need our MSDS sheets so you can provide that information to your doctor, please email us at sales@revelnail.com. Some people choose to use a face mask while applying our products and while that isn't necessary, you might want to do so. Though the FDA doesn't evaluate Dip Powders, we can tell you that traditional acrylics use EMA (Ethyl Methacrylate) and some salons even use MMA (Methyl Methacrylate - banned by FDA) as the main ingredients. Our Dip Powder doesn't use any methacrylates in our formulation. The liquids are based on Cyanoacrylate, which is similar to crazy glue or glue that's used in the hospital to seal a cut rather than getting stitches. Our Dip Powder system is safe and non-toxic, as well as Vitamin E and Calcium fortified, and they're safe even for clients with an allergy to traditional acrylics. We stand by our products with a 100% money-back guarantee, so you can be confident you're purchasing a safe product that's healthy for your nails.

templates.name: Is the Wax pen compatible with acetone?
templates.content: If the wax end of the pen is exposed to acetone, it could melt the wax off.

templates.name: What's the difference between the EZ care and Professional care?
templates.content: EZ Care has a thinner formulation for easier application. It has a slightly longer dry time as compared to Professional Care. EZ care is great for home users because it is super easy to apply and allows more time to clean up edges and cuticles whereas our Professional Care is the right choice for salon owners, nail technicians, cosmetologists, and very experienced home users.

templates.name: Do I need a nail dehydrator before using your products?
templates.content: You don't need a nail dehydrator. If your nails are oily, you can use some alcohol before application to dehydrate the nail.

templates.name: The color is wrong / looks nothing like the pictures
templates.content: We work hard to ensure our photos are a representation of the actual color you received but understand that sometimes a color looks different on your screen or against your skin tone. The true color won't show through until the activator is applied (usually the powder in the jar won't look as it does on the nail), so we find creating a swatch with 2 dips and viewing in natural light is a great way to see the true color! We also recommend an off-white natural swatch stick instead of a clear one as this provides a better representation of the true color of the product. Once you've created a swatch, and are still not happy with the color, please reach out to sales@revelnail.com with a picture of the swatch and your order number.

templates.name: Will all mystery boxes have similar colors or varieties?
templates.content: The Mystery Box for each day during the Days of Revel will be different. They'll have different colors and all are brand new. Make sure to check out our website and social media for sale notifications.

templates.name: Cuticle code not working? #2
templates.content: The code won't work with the Essential starter kit in your cart. Feel free to add a different starter kit to your cart and the oil and it should work!

templates.name: My Pro Base and Gel caps are stuck / won't open
templates.content: Pro Base and Finish Gel are strong adhesives. You need to be careful not to get them on the neck of the bottle or it will stick the cap to the bottle. To prevent this from happening in the future, please check out our IGTV video "Liquids 101" for tips and tricks for preventing your bottle from sealing shut: https://revelnail.attn.tv/sl/YpQ2X40d If you happen to get Finish Gel or Pro Base on the outside threading of your bottle, you can apply brush softener or acetone to the area (making sure not to get any inside the bottle), wait 3 minutes, then wipe the threading with a paper towel before closing the bottle.

templates.name: How many manicures/dips/applications will I get?
templates.content: You can get approximately 10-15 dips (that is, 10-15 full sets of nails) out of a .5 oz. jar, 25-30 dips from a 1 oz. jar, and 45 dips from a 2 oz. jar. You can get about 25 to 30 manicures with each 15 ml liquid bottle.

Please refresh the page, clear the cache, or use a different browser to try again. In order for the promo to work don't forget to add a Cuticle Oil to your cart as well!

templates.name: Shipping time
templates.content: Our processing time takes 3 business days and we usually ship via UPS, DHL, and FedEx. Once shipped it will take 3-8 business days domestically and 3-10 business days internationally. Please note that holidays and/or promotions may affect shipping lead time.

templates.name: How can I get mood/color changers to work?
templates.content:
Our mood changers change color based on temperature, so they change whether it's hot or cold. The bottom of the jar will show what the cold color is versus the hot color. You can test the colors by running your nails under hot or cold water. Please note that some of the shades can be a very subtle change. When we test mood changers in our lab, we use extremely hot water and extremely cold ice water to really see the change, this helps us make sure each "mood" is correct. This is why lukewarm water or your body temp may not show as much of a drastic change. While Sun Changers need a UV index of at least 4 in order for them to change. Please also note that if you are using the Gel Method, some brands of Gel liquids have "UV blockers" in their formula to prevent yellowing from the sun, this can also prevent the color change.
If you could follow up and provide a video of the manicure not changing that would be extremely helpful when we touch base with our Quality Control department to report your color complaint.

templates.name: Are dip powders hygienic/sanitary?
templates.content: Yes, dipping powders are sanitary. They don't provide an environment where bacteria can survive. In order for bacteria to spread, it must be in a moist environment. If there is any moisture on your nail when dipped into the powder, it will attract the powder to it, not the other way around. The powder won't retain moisture in the jar and therefore won't create an unsanitary environment. Dipping system pots are built for continuous safe use.

templates.name: My Glow in the Dark color isn't glowing.
templates.content: Sorry to hear that, our Firefly Collection will glow after they're charged by any light source. Sources with more UV rays (like the sun) will result in a faster charge and a stronger glow. Upon receiving your color, it may need to be more 'intentionally' charged initially, as it's been in the dark for a long time (in our warehouse and during shipping). Also note that if you're using the Gel Method, some brands of gel liquids have "UV blockers" in their formula to prevent yellowing from the sun, this can also prevent the color from glowing as brightly since the UV can't fully charge the glow pigments.

templates.name: Gel method instructions
templates.content: Use Gel Base liquid in place of Pro Base by applying a thin layer and dipping into powder, gently brushing off excess powder, then cure for 60 seconds. Repeat step 1 until you achieve the desired color payoff or thickness, then buff or file if needed. Apply Gel Base Coat again and cure for 60 seconds. Then, apply Tack-Free Gel Top coat and cure for 120 seconds.

templates.name: Instructions for gel polish manicture
templates.content: It's basically like regular Nail Polish, but you'll also need a UV lamp to cure. First, sanitize your hands, push back cuticles and remove shine from nails by using a fine drill bit or 150 grit file. Shape your nails with a 180 grit file, and using a lint-free wipe, clean the nail with alcohol. Apply a thin layer of Gel Base from the cuticle to the free edge, capping the tip. Using a LED light, cure for 30 seconds, or use a UV light to cure for 1 minute. Using gel polish color of your choice, apply a thin layer from cuticle to free edge. Be sure to cap the tips of the nail to ensure complete coverage. Cure for 60 seconds with an LED light or 2 minutes with a UV Light. Perform steps 3-4 again for each nail, and each nail should have 2 layers of gel polish. Apply a thin layer of Tack Free Gel from cuticle to free edge, and remember to cap the tips. Cure for 60 seconds with a LED light or 2 minutes with a UV Light. Then, apply Cuticle oil to the skin surrounding the nail to have a complete, nourished, and calcium-enriched gel polish manicure.

templates.name: Why does my Finish Gel have dull streak marks on the surface?
templates.content:
It is crucial to ensure you are applying enough Activator to your nail, make sure to dunk the brush back into the bottle before moving on to the next nail.

After the second coat of activator is applied, let it dry for 1-2 minutes before you apply the first coat of finish gel. The first layer of Finish Gel needs to be applied quickly, in 2-3 quick strokes. The second coat of Finish Gel can be applied more slowly and carefully and be sure to cap the front edge of the nails.

templates.name: Bubbles in Finish Gel
templates.content: If you are experiencing bubbles in your Finish Gel, you may have to "re-set" your brush for this bottle. Sometimes air bubbles get trapped in the bristles of the brush and end up in the liquid which causes your finished manicure to look like that. The best way to fix this is to take your brush out of the bottle of Finish Gel and wipe your brush in all directions on a piece of paper towel. I would recommend doing this 2-3 times. After doing that, your Finish Gel should no longer have a bubbly appearance.

templates.name: Can I exchange the Revelist Club shades?
templates.content: I'm afraid the Revelist Club shades are non-exchangeable.

templates.name: I am experiencing lifting, what’s happening?
templates.content: There's not enough Activator or improper prep. Activator is what cures the powder and makes it adhere to the nail. You might need to use more of the #3 Activator, making sure you apply to the front edge of the nail (capping the front edges). If the front edges are not capped, lifting can occur. Be sure to apply the Activator on all edges. Getting Activator on the skin will not damage the skin. You can also watch this video to learn how to properly prep the nail: https://youtu.be/-V2I3BDQJR8
